text.title: Thematic Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Thematic Analysis application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Coding MUST proceed in two passes: open coding (per-line tags, no scheme) -> axial coding (collapse into themes). Single-pass coding is rejected because it bakes in interpretation before the data has spoken.
rule.rationale: Two-pass coding is the established qualitative standard (Braun & Clarke); skipping the open pass causes researchers to map data to their hypothesis rather than the other way round.

rule.statement: Two independent coders MUST tag >=20% of the corpus separately; Cohen's kappa across their codes MUST be >= 0.6. Below 0.6 = reconcile codebook and re-code before continuing.
rule.rationale: Single-coder qualitative work is indistinguishable from confirmation bias; the kappa gate forces disagreement to surface and be resolved.

rule.statement: Coding stops only after 3 consecutive new transcripts produce zero new themes (saturation). Stopping at a fixed N without saturation evidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Fixed-N stopping rules under-sample rare themes and over-sample common ones; saturation is the empirically-defended rule from grounded-theory practice.

rule.statement: Every theme in the final report MUST be anchored to >=2 direct verbatim quotes with transcript-id + timestamp/line-number. Themes without anchors are stripped.
rule.rationale: Un-anchored themes drift into the analyst's prior beliefs; quote anchoring makes the report falsifiable by the reader.

rule.statement: The report MUST emit a per-theme backlog row: theme_id, severity (low/med/high), proposed action, evidence count, owner. Reports without a backlog hand-off are rejected as documentation rather than insight.
rule.rationale: Insight is action that did not happen yet; the backlog row turns the theme into a unit of work.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
